barRaise.topFinding: Twenty-seventh review, eighth straight healthy verdict. HEAD is still f61c179 (v0.51.3, 06-15 09:16), so the code board is byte-for-byte unchanged from the last seven reviews: zero HIGH, two AI-owned MEDIUMs that neither block 1.0, and four LOWs. The only working-tree movement is the same cosmetic uncommitted edits carried since 06-16 (a CLAUDE.md CONTEXT.md size note plus four comment-only stylesheet repoints in src/mobile-hooks*.ts), re-confirmed cosmetic via git diff --stat (6 insertions, 6 deletions). The lone item that moved is observability, and it moved the wrong way an eighth time: today's canonical audit (docs/CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-06-22.md) ran clean ("No drift"), the eighth clean audit in a row, but Chains.json audit.latestReportPath still points at docs/CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-06-14.md. That pointer is now eight audits stale (06-15 through 06-22 all clean on disk), up from seven on 06-21. The gap widens by one every day the daily audit fires and no release does, because the dashboard refresh is welded to release time. It stays MEDIUM and benign only because all eight skipped audits are genuinely clean. Decoupling that refresh remains the highest-value, slowest-compounding, AI-ownable fix on the board before 1.0.

actions.title: Decouple the dashboard audit block from release time: have the daily canonical-audit writer parse the high/medium/low counts from the latest CANONICAL_AUDIT-*.md TL;DR and advance audit.latestReportPath, so a between-release HIGH cannot sit invisible. The pointer is now eight audits stale (06-14 on the JSON, clean 06-15 through 06-22 on disk). Highest-value AI-ownable chore on the board.
Post-1.0, split the worker/src/index.js monolith into modules. No pre-1.0 action.
